title: Handle COPY --chown in Dockerfile
body:
Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

This change will allow the Dockfile copy command like:
```
COPY --chown=3267:3267 tom.txt /tmp
```
to work appropriately.  

This addresses #407

Test log:
```
# cat ~/Dockerfile
FROM fedora
COPY --chown=3267:3267 tom.txt /tmp 
CMD /bin/bash

# cat tom.txt
Fake file for copy

# buildah bud -t tom -f ~/Dockerfile .
STEP 1: FROM fedora
STEP 2: COPY --chown=3267:3267 tom.txt /tmp 
STEP 3: CMD /bin/bash
STEP 4: COMMIT containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage:overlay.override_kernel_check=true]localhost/tom:latest
Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:891e1e4ef82ad02a4ea1f030831f942d722c7694c4db64ca3239c8163b811c58
Copying blob sha256:f4a94b976a9b13731bd63356b3740a8ff606c5a0450991ca8badca26ba5c5272
 162 B / 162 B [============================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:179a2513ebda9bd6da61eec8db5919b3e20f2bbad4377f5df0f2f2c030632312
 1.60 KiB / 1.60 KiB [======================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 179a2513ebda9bd6da61eec8db5919b3e20f2bbad4377f5df0f2f2c030632312

# buildah from tom
tom-working-container

# buildah run tom-working-container /bin/bash

[root@mrsdalloway /]# cat /tmp/tom.txt
Fake file for copy

[root@mrsdalloway /]# ls -alF /tmp/tom.txt
-rw-r--r--. 1 3267 3267 19 Aug 11 19:09 /tmp/tom.txt
```

comments.body: @TomSweeneyRedHat Add some tests for this.  As well as vendor in your changes to imagebuilder to make sure this is working correctly.
@rhatdan ack, will do as soon as the imagebuilder PR gets merged.
Drats, forgot to include the new tests/bud/copy-chown dir in the last push, trying again.

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan e29e951d0c1b1041a4aa8be523ddf8d86e196828 -->
:hourglass: Testing commit e29e951 with merge cbf6c97...
CRUD! Failing in the new test, but it passes in my sandbox.  Looking...

I'm not sure why, but the first part of the 'ls-alF' output was different in Travis than on my test machine.  I've ripped that out of the test and am now using the stat command to verify the uid/gid of the copied file in the container.  Fingers crossed that this turns the trick.
New test passed, but now we've a net hiccup in an unrelated test.  Restarting the test.
bot's on vacation, closing and will reopen.
